verses.text: When the king's anger cools and he misses Vashti, his servants propose gathering the fairest virgins of the realm. A new search begins. Human schemes for the king's pleasure become, all unknowing, the path by which God places His chosen one.

When the king's anger cools and he misses Vashti, his servants propose gathering the fairest virgins of the realm. A new search begins. Human schemes for the king's pleasure become, all unknowing, the path by which God places His chosen one.

Mordecai, a Jew of the captivity in Shushan, has raised his orphaned cousin Esther, beautiful in form and face. A faithful guardian shelters a parentless child. God often prepares His instruments in obscure homes and quiet kindnesses long before their hour.

Mordecai, a Jew of the captivity in Shushan, has raised his orphaned cousin Esther, beautiful in form and face. A faithful guardian shelters a parentless child. God often prepares His instruments in obscure homes and quiet kindnesses long before their hour.

Mordecai, a Jew of the captivity in Shushan, has raised his orphaned cousin Esther, beautiful in form and face. A faithful guardian shelters a parentless child. God often prepares His instruments in obscure homes and quiet kindnesses long before their hour.

Esther is taken to the palace, wins the keeper's favour, and, as Mordecai charged, keeps her people secret, while he watches daily for her welfare. She is favoured yet still guided. God's providence places her, and a guardian's care still surrounds her.

Each maiden is prepared a full year before her single night with the king. The pomp of the harem masks an empty splendour. Worldly favour is fleeting and uncertain, but God is steering all toward an end none of them can see.

Each maiden is prepared a full year before her single night with the king. The pomp of the harem masks an empty splendour. Worldly favour is fleeting and uncertain, but God is steering all toward an end none of them can see.

Each maiden is prepared a full year before her single night with the king. The pomp of the harem masks an empty splendour. Worldly favour is fleeting and uncertain, but God is steering all toward an end none of them can see.

Esther, asking for nothing beyond what was advised, wins the favour of all who see her, and the king loves her above the rest and sets the royal crown on her head. Her modesty draws every heart. God can lift the humble to the throne in His own time.

Esther still keeps her kindred secret, obeying Mordecai as she did when a child in his house. Crowned, she remains teachable. True greatness does not outgrow the wise counsel and humble obedience that shaped it.

Esther still keeps her kindred secret, obeying Mordecai as she did when a child in his house. Crowned, she remains teachable. True greatness does not outgrow the wise counsel and humble obedience that shaped it.

Mordecai uncovers a plot of two officers against the king, warns Esther, and the traitors are hanged, the deed recorded in the chronicles. A loyal act seems to pass unrewarded. But God writes it down for the right hour, and nothing done in faithfulness is truly forgotten.
